reason: Package 5 of the UI/UX consolidation approved in UI-UX-SPEC.md (see DECISIONS.md, 2026-07-12). It replaces the way a broken target (its folder or file is gone) is shown on the wheel: instead of dimming the whole tile to 40% opacity — which read like a rendering bug and offered no action — the tile now stays full opacity, its icon goes grayscale, and a small yellow "!" mark sits in the bottom-right corner; a left-click opens a Locate…/Remove mini-menu (Remove deletes with an Undo toast) instead of silently failing to launch. The exact indication (warning corner mark + grayscale + Locate/Remove menu) is the one the owner already approved in the spec's tile-indication table, so this is applying an approved decision, not a new visual proposal; no per-package mockup adds anything. The tile order was reviewed and left as-is: TargetStore.OrderedForDisplay is already a persistent function of the list, pins and TilePosition, so the spec's 'persistent order, even distribution, growth at the end' requirement is already met and rewriting it would only risk regressions.
approvedBy: Ivan Larin (repo owner) via chat request on 2026-07-12
